spec_match.rationale: The demo models wrap versus balance and now degrades safely, but native balanced wrapping could not be observed because this Headless Chrome 149 build reports CSS.supports('flex-wrap: balance') as false.

interactive.rationale: Mode buttons, the native width slider, and all four scenario buttons now use working in-scope listeners and update the toolbar, comparison, width, and statistics.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The toolbar and side-by-side comparison directly illustrate the orphaned-last-row problem described by the feature page.

edge_cases.rationale: Widths from 200–800 px, four preset viewports, wrap/balance modes, and an explicit unsupported-browser fallback cover the important layout and compatibility branches.

references.rationale: The parent feature page links ChromeStatus feature 4547107962486784 and the CSS Flexbox Level 2 specification used by the conformance suite.

conformance.rationale: The live suite passes 3/5 assertions; the two balance-specific parser/computed-style assertions fail because the installed Chrome 149 build lacks the feature, while legacy flex-wrap contracts remain green.

runtime_evidence.rationale: DevTools MCP reproduced three ReferenceErrors from the old inline handlers, then verified both mode buttons, slider fill/ArrowRight, and all preset-width controls with clean console output after repair.

telemetry_health.rationale: Post-repair interactions generate no runtime, console, network, or assertion errors, and unsupported behavior is surfaced as explicit page state rather than an exception.

design_system.rationale: The fallback notice and controls use shared color, typography, border, and background variables, with text explaining state rather than relying on color alone.

accessibility.rationale: Mode buttons expose aria-pressed state, the unsupported explanation becomes the balance button's accessible description, the slider is labelled and keyboard-operable, and changing layout statistics are exposed through a polite status region.

openQuestions.title: Re-run the native balance layout in a supporting Chrome build
openQuestions.detail: The installed Headless Chrome 149 build does not parse flex-wrap: balance. Re-test the same widths in a build where both balance conformance assertions pass and capture the native line distribution before declaring the visual comparison fully verified.

summary: The sweep found that every visible control was wired through inline attributes to functions scoped inside an IIFE, producing setMode, updateWidth, and setWidth ReferenceErrors. The controls now use addEventListener, expose pressed/live state, and feature-detect balance. Unsupported browsers receive a visible, accessible wrap fallback instead of a misleading single-line overflow.
